So the dominant and most important aspect touched in the second FGD which is professional exams preparation was the pressure that these people feel and and how they cope with it. I mean

The pressure everybody's feeling is immense. they get, you know, back-to-back failures and one guy even said till 12th standard you don't know what failure is because you are passing every exam and suddenly after 12th exam there is something called not, you know, passing or not getting through, not being successful and that is very difficult to deal with.

and that guy was earlier preparing for CA and now preparing for CAT. young fella. he said I I tried foundation four times and I didn't clear it. and that that got too much that failure got too much of of of it. and then he decided to get into CAT.

so the dominant, you know, conversation and the most important aspect which was not touched as comprehensively in any other FGDs is, you know, the pressure of preparation, the pressure of failure, the

girl in the second FGD, wonderful lady. she is the only female in her entire family, you know, even extended family who has gone out of the house to Delhi to prepare for CAT.

Uh she wanted to do uh NDA. Uh appeared also but was conference out. And then she wants to do CDS now after her graduation is complete. But her brother elder brother uh cleared CAT and is studying at some IIM.

Uh of course she said it is not safe at night. Uh but in the day time she enjoys uh the city. But uh the the other thing is that she is under immense pressure. She has two younger siblings. And uh even her brother who cleared CAT in the third attempt has called up and said that if you don't clear CAT uh in November then by March you will be married off. And her father also is on the same page.

Uh, she said she feels like this is the only opportunity she has and it's not only for her but also for her siblings, younger siblings. Uh so the pressure she feels is immense and the way she deals with it is she said I try and study more uh than I can.
